data.body: AC3 BACK-OUT TESTED LIVE (reversible). (1) ACL revoke restores single-change flow — TESTED: pushed refs/meta/config removing 'pushMerge = group feature-branch-drivers' from [access refs/for/refs/heads/main] (keeping exclusiveGroupPermissions so no inherited leak); a --no-ff merge push to refs/for/main by JoeOakhartNava (a feature-branch-driver who merged change 254 earlier) was then REFUSED 'commit 7451b07: you are not allowed to upload merges' -> single-change-only restored. Then restored the grant via a forward commit (project.config byte-identical to snapshot f8bd6ac8; both pushMerge grants back); a merge push SUCCEEDS again (change 282, abandoned). Fully reversible, zero residual config change. (2) Submit-type revert VERIFIED vs S1 prior — LIVE read GET /a/projects/rebar/config: submit_type=MERGE_IF_NECESSARY, use_content_merge configured=TRUE/inherited=True -> matches S1-recorded MERGE_IF_NECESSARY + content-merge true, so deleting the [submit] block is a proven no-op. (3) Bot rollback path DOCUMENTED (two-vote-gate-rollback.md §A.2 pt4 + review-bot-ops.md :prev rollback) — and exercised live this session (the review-bot fix, bug pelt-mead-aeon). Note: setup-project.sh (audited apply path) needs an admin SSH key absent here, so the test used a direct admin HTTPS refs/meta/config push + snapshot restore.
